title: ‘Quality of life is plummeting’: Readers share their economic concerns
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A MassINC poll of 854 Massachusetts residents found 42% feel financially worse off than a year ago.
Readers cite taxes, unaffordable housing, and healthcare costs as major economic concerns affecting quality of life.
Boston.com readers say the middle class in Massachusetts requires $97,000-$112,000 income to maintain status.

title: A ‘lifestyle-focused’ restaurant opens Friday at this new Cape Cod hotel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Gypsy Sol, a lifestyle-focused restaurant, opens May 15 at The Uncommoner Hotel in West Yarmouth, Cape Cod.
The restaurant offers craft cocktails, small plates, tarot readings, sound baths, and wellness events for community and guests.
Chef Mark Cina's menu includes seafood, Mediterranean dishes, and zodiac-inspired cocktails; Arcana Cafe offers coffee and pastries.

title: 18-year-old arrested in connection to Mother’s Day double shooting in Dorchester
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jaynell Sumler, 18, was arrested for involvement in a double shooting in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
The incident at 69 Kingsdale St. left a woman seriously injured and a man with non-life-threatening injuries.
Sumler faces multiple charges, including two counts of assault to murder and firearm violations.

title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’ was renewed for Season 2
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' was renewed for a second season, Bravo announced on May 11.
The show became Bravo's biggest series premiere episode on Peacock and is the network's second-biggest show across platforms.
The series has two nominations for the 2026 Critics Choice Real TV Awards, including Best Unstructured Series.

title: Alleged Memorial Drive shooter pleads not guilty from hospital bed
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Brown, 46, pleads not guilty to assault and firearms charges remotely from his hospital bed.
Brown was shot after a confrontation with a state trooper; two victims sustained life-threatening injuries.
Prosecutors detailed Brown's criminal history, lack of firearm license, and recent release from a psychiatric facility.

title: MassINC poll: Healey’s approval down as excitement over T improvements wanes
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MassINC poll reveals 82% of Massachusetts voters rate the transit system as fair or poor, affecting public satisfaction.
Governor Maura Healey's approval rating falls to 39-45%, influenced by voter dissatisfaction with state transit conditions.
Support for microtransit services grows, with two-thirds favoring increased state funding to assist underserved communities.

title: Amelia’s Taqueria in Allston has permanently closed
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Amelia's Taqueria closed its Allston location on Brighton Avenue permanently after 14 years on May 1, 2026.
The closure notice thanked customers and directed them to other locations on Beacon Street, Boylston Street, and Assembly Row.
The Allston taqueria faced challenges, including a 2022 fire that temporarily shut it down before reopening in May 2025.

title: Teen arrested after allegedly leaving JP school with loaded firearm
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 16-year-old arrested in Jamaica Plain is facing delinquency charges after allegedly carrying a loaded handgun.
The teenager fled after being seen with a gun near Glen Road and disposed of it under a white SUV.
He will be arraigned in West Roxbury District Juvenile Court for firearm possession and related charges.

title: Red Sox ace Garrett Crochet provided an injury update after bullpen session
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Garrett Crochet conducted a 20-pitch bullpen session, marking his first mound action since shoulder inflammation in April.
Crochet aims to skip a rehab start, believing he's ready to contribute directly to the Red Sox rotation.
A return timeline for Crochet is currently undefined as he focuses on regaining shoulder health and improving mechanics.

title: 1 dead, 3 injured in late-night boat crash at Logan Airport pier
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Elizabeth Dankert, 24, died after a boat crashed into a pier at Logan Airport, with three others injured.
The boat, owned by a 40-year-old man, struck Pier 4R at about 11:20 p.m., causing passengers to be thrown onto rocks.
The crash is under investigation by Massachusetts State Police; the injured parties' conditions are not life-threatening.
